library_preparation_preflight.summary: The artifact is a Maven Resolver provider: its main artifact supplies Maven-specific descriptor/version readers and session utilities, but documentation and source state that clients must add repository connectors/transports before real repository resolution works. Meaningful hermetic coverage should use a temporary file-based Maven repository, which requires extra Resolver connector and file transport test dependencies.

deterministic_setup.reason: Maven 3.9.12 manages Resolver 1.9.25, and MavenRepositorySystemUtils only registers provider services; artifact/metadata resolution needs a RepositoryConnectorFactory.
Hermetic tests can exercise descriptor/version resolution against a temporary file:// Maven repository without live network access, but Resolver needs a file transporter implementation for that.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Use a temporary local repository/session and a temporary file:// remote repository with minimal POM/metadata fixtures. Configure the RepositorySystem from MavenRepositorySystemUtils.newServiceLocator(), register the available connector/transport services, and set a LocalRepositoryManager rooted in a temp directory. Avoid live Maven Central/network resolution. Avoid covering MavenResolverModule unless explicit Guice/Guava optional dependencies are intentionally added.
risks: Without connector and transport implementations, tests that call RepositorySystem resolution APIs will fail with missing connector/transporter services rather than exercising this provider.

The public MavenResolverModule class references optional Guice classes that are not part of the normal transitive runtime classpath; generated tests should avoid it unless additional optional dependencies are added.

HTTP transport is not included because hermetic tests should use file:// repositories; tests that choose live HTTP resolution would need org.apache.maven.resolver:maven-resolver-transport-http:1.9.25 and would be less reliable.
